title: [FIRRTL] connection of consts verifier not flow sensitive
body:
I believe we are rejecting valid usages of const.
```
firrtl.circuit "Foo" {
  firrtl.module @Foo(in %in   : !firrtl.bundle<a flip: uint<1>>,
                     out %out : !firrtl.bundle<a flip: const.uint<1>>) {
    firrtl.connect %out, %in : !firrtl.bundle<a flip: const.uint<1>>, !firrtl.bundle<a flip: uint<1>>
  }
}
```
Should be valid FIRRTL, but we are getting:
```
./bin/firtool ./test2.mlir 
./test2.mlir:4:5: error: type mismatch between destination '!firrtl.bundle<a flip: const.uint<1>>' and source '!firrtl.bundle<a flip: uint<1>>'
    firrtl.connect %out, %in : !firrtl.bundle<a flip: const.uint<1>>, !firrtl.bundle<a flip: uint<1>>
    ^
./test2.mlir:4:5: note: see current operation: "firrtl.connect"(%arg1, %arg0) : (!firrtl.bundle<a flip: const.uint<1>>, !firrtl.bundle<a flip: uint<1>>) -> ()
```

I believe the issue is that the verifier is not swapping the dest and src when there is a flip. Maybe need to do it here: https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLTypes.cpp#L697-L698

If I remove the `const` from the types, I get the following Verilog:
```verilog
module Foo(
  input  out_a,
  output in_a
);

  assign in_a = out_a;
endmodule
```
